title: Isaac Newton
ground_truth_corrupted_sentence: In addition to his creation of calculus, Newton's work on mathematics was largely confined to applied problems.
guessed_corrupted_sentence: He was also the first to explain the Magnus effect.

parsed_response.corrupted_sentence: He was also the first to explain the Magnus effect.
parsed_response.explanation: The Magnus effect — the lateral force on a spinning body moving through a fluid — is named after Heinrich Gustav Magnus, who reported and described the phenomenon in 1852, well after Newton's lifetime. Newton did not explain this effect; modern explanations rely on later developments in fluid dynamics (viscous flow and boundary-layer theory) that were not available to Newton. Because the sentence attributes a discovery and explanation to Newton that historically belongs to 19th‑century scientists, it is the incorrect (substituted) sentence in the article.
